items.title: Multilayer Perceptron
items.description: The MLP is the feedforward architecture UAT applies to — stacked affine layers plus non-linear activations. MLP owns forward pass, training, and backprop; this topic owns the expressivity theorem and its limits.

items.title: Underfitting vs overfitting
items.description: UAT explains that a large enough MLP can fit complex patterns (avoiding pure linear underfitting), but excess capacity on limited data causes overfitting — low train error with high validation error. This topic states expressivity; underfitting/overfitting owns train–val diagnostics and fixes.

items.title: Generalization
items.description: UAT concerns representing a function on a compact region, not performing well on unseen (x, y). Generalization owns unseen-data performance, the generalization gap, and the i.i.d. assumption — the main capability UAT does not promise.

items.title: ReLU and variants
items.description: Non-linear activations (ReLU, sigmoid, tanh) are prerequisites for UAT — without them, depth collapses to linear maps. ReLU and variants owns activation definitions and behavior; this topic references them as the non-linearity that makes universal approximation possible.

items.title: Layers and neurons
items.description: Width and depth vocabulary used when discussing shallow-wide vs deep-narrow UAT and architecture trade-offs. Layers and neurons owns structural definitions; this topic applies them to approximation capacity.
